1: A shadow: "Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion, which cannot be moved, but abides forever." Matthew 7:24-25 — the one who builds on Christ's words stands like a house on rock when the floods come. 1 Corinthians 3:11 — "no one can lay a foundation other than that which is laid, which is Jesus Christ." The immovability comes from the foundation, not the builder.
2: A shadow: "As the mountains surround Jerusalem, so the LORD surrounds his people." John 10:28-29 — "I give them eternal life, and they shall never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand. My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them out of the Father's hand." Surrounded by Christ on one side and the Father on the other — a double enclosure that cannot be broken.
3: A shadow: "the scepter of wickedness shall not rest on the land allotted to the righteous." 1 John 3:8 — "The reason the Son of God appeared was to destroy the works of the devil." The scepter of wickedness that would rest on God's people is the power Christ came to overthrow — Colossians 2:15 ("having disarmed the rulers and authorities, he made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them by the cross").
4: A revelation of God: "Do good, O LORD, to those who are good and to those who are upright in their hearts." The justice of God in rewarding the righteous finds its ultimate expression in Christ's imputed righteousness — the "good" ones are those clothed in Christ's righteousness (2 Corinthians 5:21). Christ the judge will say to his sheep, "Come, you who are blessed by my Father" (Matthew 25:34).
5: A revelation of God: "those who turn aside to their crooked ways the LORD will lead away with evildoers." Matthew 25:41 — "Depart from me, you cursed, into the eternal fire." Christ executes the final separation that the psalm anticipates. The psalm's "Peace be upon Israel!" is the peace that Christ pronounces over those who are his own (John 20:19-21; Ephesians 2:17).
